item.title: Lebanon govt, Hezbollah divided over talks with Israel
item.description: Lebanon’s president said Monday direct negotiations with Israel aimed to end the Israel-Hezbollah war, adding that those who dragged Lebanon to war were the ones committing ‘treason’ — a jab at Hezbollah, whose leader called the talks a ‘sin’....

item.title: Iran blames US for talks failure
item.description: Iran’s top diplomat blamed Washington on Monday for the failure of Middle East peace talks during a visit to Russia, where president Vladimir Putin promised him Moscow’s support in bringing the war to a close....

item.title: Azmi tells ICT Tk 4cr he got during interim govt house rent, not compensation
item.description: Retired Brigadier General Abdullahil Aman Azmi on Monday told a tribunal that he received Tk 4 crore from the army as house rent, not as compensation during the interim government after he was freed from a Directorate General of Forces Intelligence’s secret detention centre....Politics

item.title: DU teacher sent to jail over student’s death
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday sent Dhaka University associate professor Sudip Chakraborty to jail in a case filed over the alleged abetment of the suicide of student Munira Mahjabin Mimo....

item.title: Woman’s body recovered at Mohammadpur
item.description: The police early Monday recovered the body of a woman from a house at Nabinagar Housing in the capital’s Mohammadpur area....

item.title: Dhaka expresses solidarity with KSA
item.description: Bangladesh has expressed solidarity with the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia amid the conflict across the Arab Gulf states following the United States and Israel’s joint attack on Iran and Tehran’s retaliatory strikes on Israel and the US bases in the region since February 28....

item.title: UK pledges support for Bangladesh’s democratic journey
item.description: British high commissioner to Bangladesh Sarah Cook on Monday said that the United Kingdom would continue to stand by the country in its democratic journey....Geographic Reference

item.title: ACC seeks info on ex-high commissioner Saida Muna, husband
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Commission has sent letters to several offices seeking documents of former Bangladesh high commissioner to the United Kingdom Saida Muna Tasneem and her husband over embezzling nearly Tk 2,000 crore and laundering the money abroad....

item.title: Man with speech disabilities held over mother’s killing in Natore
item.description: The police detained a man with speech disabilities over the alleged killing of his mother at Mallikhati Ghoshpara area in Natore on Sunday night. ...

item.title: DCMC dental unit succeeds in professional exams
item.description: Students of Dhaka Community Medical College and its dental unit achieved remarkable success in the recently published November 2025 first and third professional examinations held under Dhaka University....

item.title: Gonoshasthaya Kendra celebrates 54th founding anniv
item.description: The Gonoshasthaya Kendra celebrated its 54th founding anniversary at its PHA Bhaban of Savar in Dhaka on Monday....Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: BMU observes World Intellectual Property Day
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University observed World Intellectual Property Day 2026 on its campus in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: Govt urges KSA to simplify recruiting agency registration
item.description: Expatriates’ welfare and overseas employment minister Ariful Haque Chowdhury at a meeting with Saudi Arabia ambassador to Bangladesh Abdullah Zafer bin Abiyah on Monday proposed simplifying the registration process of recruiting agencies at the......

item.title: Left parties issues ultimatum to scrap deal by April 30
item.description: The Anti-Fascist and Anti-War Alliance on Monday called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to scrap the ‘unequal’ trade deal signed with the United States by the immediate past interim government....

item.title: Bangladesh urges US to ensure safety, security of its citizens
item.description: Bangladesh has urged the United States to ensure the safety and security of Bangladeshi citizens and students staying there, following the killing of two Bangladeshi students....

item.title: Farm loan overdue doubles
item.description: Overdue agricultural loans in Bangladesh's banking sector doubled in the first eight months of the current fiscal year......

item.title: UNCTAD for a national investment policy
item.description: The United Nations Conference on Trade and Development recommended that Bangladesh introduce a national investment policy and a consolidated investment law......

item.title: Former SIBL owners seek to regain control
item.description: Former owners of Social Islami Bank have moved to reclaim control of the troubled lender under the controversial Section 18(Ka) of the new Bank Resolution Act, 2026, which allows previous shareholders to regain ownership of banks placed under resolution......

item.title: India names Dinesh Trivedi as new envoy to Bangladesh
item.description: India has appointed Dinesh Trivedi as the next high commissioner of India to Bangladesh....

item.title: High taxes, policy instability blamed for industrial slow growth
item.description: Businesses in the steel and re-rolling sector urged the government to simplify and rationalise the tariff and value-added tax regime in the upcoming national budget for the financial year 2026-27, warning that high taxes and frequent policy shifts are constraining industrial growth......

item.title: NBR to introduce QR codes on cigarette packs
item.description: The National Board of Revenue is set to introduce QR codes on cigarette packets and offer rewards for reporting tax evasion, as part of efforts to curb illicit trade and strengthen revenue collection ahead of the next fiscal year’s budget......

item.title: AMTOB urges sweeping telecom tax cuts to boost digital access
item.description: Association of Mobile Telecom Operators of Bangladesh on Monday called for major tax reforms in the telecommunications sector, warning that the current fiscal regime is undermining industry sustainability and slowing digital inclusion......

item.title: UGC stresses basic training for university teachers
item.description: Chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Mamun Ahmed has said that the four-month residential basic training will play an important role in enhancing the capacity of new university teachers and building a......Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: BNP activist shot dead at Raujan in Ctg
item.description: A Bangladesh Nationalist Party activist was shot dead by unidentified miscreants at Raujan upazila in Chattogram on Sunday night....

item.title: China vows countermeasures against ‘Made in Europe’ plan
item.description: Beijing slammed on Monday an EU plan aimed to bolster the bloc’s industries against fierce competition from China, vowing countermeasures if it is enacted......

item.title: Limits of coal-dependent energy path
item.description: AT A time of growing volatility in global energy markets, energy security has become an increasingly pressing concern for Bangladesh. Disruptions in international fuel supply chains and price fluctuations have exposed the country’s structural dependence on imported energy. The effects are already visible domestically, with fuel shortages, rising costs and pressure on......

item.title: Last fiscal test before LDC graduation
item.description: THE upcoming national budget for fiscal year 2026–27 is not just another fiscal exercise. It is the final budget before Bangladesh exits the Least Developed Country category on November 24, 2026. This transition will mark the end of preferential treatment and the beginning of a more competitive economic reality. The question is no longer about growth alone, but about......

item.title: Gig workers without a net
Measles surge signals deeper failures
item.description: WORLD Immunisation Week (24–30 April 2026) is being observed this year under the banner ‘For every generation, vaccines work.’ The theme marks 52 years of the Expanded Programme on Immunisation and its role in saving an estimated 154 million lives since 1974. Yet in Bangladesh, the moment feels more like a warning. As the world reflects on decades of......

item.title: Changing gulf order
item.description: THE unfolding conflict in the Middle East in 2026 is far more than a regional military escalation. It reflects a deeper convergence of long historical memory, ideological conviction and contemporary power politics. As tensions between Iran and the United States–Israel axis move beyond episodic confrontation, the crisis threatens to reshape the fragile balance of the......

item.title: Delay in probe undermines journalists’ safety concern
item.description: THE high-powered task force set up to complete the investigation of the murder of journalists Sagar Sarwar and Meherun Runi now follows the same legacy that such investigations did during fallen Awami League regime. The journalist couple was found dead in their flat in Dhaka on February 11, 2012. And, the police investigation report is yet to be forthcoming. Soon......

item.title: Accountability deferred in seven-murder case
item.description: FAMILIES of the seven people abducted and killed in Narayanganj and Narsingdi wait for justice even 12 years after the incidents. This stands as an unambiguous indictment of the justice system. The crime shocked the nation. A sitting city councillor, a lawyer and others were abducted and murdered and their bodies were discarded into a river. What made the case more......

item.title: UAE slams Gulf allies as ‘weak’ on Iran
item.description: A senior United Arab Emirates official on Monday criticised the country’s Gulf allies over their response to Iranian retaliatory attacks in the region following the Israeli-US strikes that launched the Middle East war....Sports news updates

item.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills 14
item.description: A small plane crashed Monday in South Sudan near the capital Juba, killing all 13 passengers and the pilot, local officials said....

item.title: Global military spending surges on insecurity: report
item.description: Global military spending reached nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking an 11th consecutive year of growth, researchers said Monday, as insecurity and rearmament fuelled defence budgets....

item.title: Amending constitution only viable path to implement July Charter: chief whip
item.description: Chief whip Nurul Islam Moni has said that amending the constitution is the only viable path to implementing the commitments of the July National Charter, as the country navigates apoliticaltransition that requires both reform and legal continuity....

item.title: Ex-DGFI director Afzal Naser put on fresh remand in Hasan killing case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday placed former DGFI director and dismissed lieutenant colonel Afzal Naser on a fresh two-day remand in Mahmudul Hasan murder case....Politics

item.title: C’nawabganj filling station fined over alleged attempt to smuggle diesel
item.description: A mobile court fined a filling station at Shibganj in Chapainawabganj on Sunday night on charges of attempting to illegally smuggle diesel....

item.title: 3 BNP, JCD leaders suspended over attack on Netrakona Jamaat MP’s vehicle
item.description: Three leaders of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and its student wing Chhatra Dal have been suspended over the attack on the vehicle of Jamaat lawmaker, Netrakona 5, Masum Mostafa....

item.title: Coast Guard to celebrate 31st founding anniv today
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard is scheduled to celebrate its 31st founding anniversary at its headquarters at Agargaon in Dhaka today....

item.title: Missing imam’s body recovered in Chandpur river
item.description: The body of a mosque imam was recovered a day after he went missing while bathing in the Dakatia River on the outskirts of Chandpur town....Geographic Reference

item.title: RAB arrests five suspected muggers over customs officer killing
item.description: Five suspected members of a mugger gang were arrested on Sunday night over the killing of customs official Bullet Bairagi from different spots in Cumilla, two days after he had gone missing while returning to the district from Chattogram....

item.title: Two arrested over lynching of Kushtia pir
item.description: Two youths were arrested early Monday in connection with the lynching of pir Abdur Rahman at Daulatpur upazila in Kushtia, 16 days after his brutal killing....

item.title: Transport workers stage demo in Rajshahi over attack
item.description: A group of transport workers formed a human chain in Rajshahi city on Monday, protesting against an attack on them allegedly carried out by individuals they described as associates of the leaders of the Awami League....

item.title: Students’ group, JCD face off over student politics at RUET
item.description: A group of students of Rajshahi University of Engineering and Technology and activists from the Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, engaged in protests and counter-protests on Monday over attempts to resume studentpoliticson campus....Bangladesh political commentary

item.title: DIESEL CRISIS: Chuadanga farmers fear crop loss
item.description: A severe diesel shortage, compounded by a dry spell and intense heatwave, has disrupted irrigation in Chuadanga, threatening to damage boro and other seasonal crops....
